behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural MT model output quality (fluency/semantic preservation), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by module wiring alone.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus-based evaluation against the real translation API using bilingual reference sets and semantic-adequacy checks (e.g., human-rated or model-based adequacy/fluency thresholds). Fail if coherence/meaning scores drop below threshold.

behaviors.description: On upstream 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and still returns success.
behaviors.justification: A real failure can occur at HTTP boundary handling (status mapping, retry/error parsing). Mocking the API client can hide this; exercising real HTTP response behavior is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service with a real local SQLite fallback DB and point primary translator client to a local HTTP test server that returns 503. Call translation endpoint and assert: no caller error, fallback translation returned, and fallback path telemetry/log event emitted.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for known phrases in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is query/ORM/encoding interaction with SQLite storage, not just pure lookup logic. Unit mocks can miss SQL/schema/collation issues.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temp SQLite database with seeded phrase rows, invoke fallback translator through repository/service boundary, and verify exact expected translation for existing phrases (including Unicode/diacritics cases).

behaviors.description: Quality tracker computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: The behavior includes both computation and persistence. Real failures often happen in handoff/type/storage (wrong field, transaction, truncation), which unit-only tests miss.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger translation flow with both outputs available, then assert persisted quality record contains the exact expected character-level edit distance for known string pairs and is linked to the correct request/translation IDs.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary output and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is orchestration across modules (response selection + background task enqueue/execution + metric write). This is cross-module async behavior but not inherently external-system dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service components in-process (primary/fallback adapters, async job executor, SQLite metric store). Call translate endpoint and assert immediate response equals primary translation; then wait/poll until background quality metric record appears.
